items.caption_text:
HCL tech shares hit a fresh 52 week low this week and got hit hard post very weak earnings. 

With AI disruption, delay in decision making from large clients and deal cancellations , the sector is under pressure 

A few weeks ago I spoke to Kenneth Andrade of Old Bridge Capital on why he exited IT stocks completely earlier this year from his portfolio 

“We dont know whats happening in the tech sector. will we know in the next quarter ?  maybe, maybe not. 
If i had to build a portfolio today , I would build it without tech names” he says.

He explained that the terminal value of the IT businesses are falling and there is a lot of uncertainty in whether these businesses can continue to grow in the same way as earlier.

Spent a couple of days at the beautiful wellness centre @atmantan a few weeks ago. 

It was my first time at any wellness retreat. Thank you to the gracious hosts and founders for inviting me. @sharmilee_agrawal_kapur ❤️ 

Wellness has always felt like a privilege . You need time , access and most importantly, money. But in today’s fast paced , over stimulated world, it now feels like a necessity . 

The wellness industry in India is growing at a fast pace , it’s now a 160 bln $ industry in India and I was quite surprised to see how packed this place was. Everything from physical therapy, nutrition , spiritual healing , medically supervised diet programs it was all under one roof. 

A first time for me , the no sugar less salt diet was a bit tough 😅 but overall a great experience 💓

women often tell me that they are “not good with money” and struggle with basic financial decisions. One of the big reasons could be internalised patriarchy. 

Internalised patriarchy is real , it’s powerful , and often invisible. The belief that you are not capable of taking big financial decisions or handling money. 

women unconsciously adopt beliefs that limit their own financial power

The subconscious adoption of traditional gender roles that prioritize male financial control, devalue women's earning power, and perpetuate economic dependence. 

Unlearning years of social conditioning around money and finance is the first step to financial freedom as women 🎀

Should you invest now or not? 

I know there is a ceasefire and markets have seen a big relief rally so the temptation is to invest now . But chasing headlines and being trigger happy can affect your portfolio in the long run. This is a volatile market and as we know anything can happen anytime.

So what do you do ? 

Whenever I think of putting fresh money to work I remember the wise words of veteran investor Sanjoy Bhattacharya who told me Sonia ask yourself three questions whenever you want to take a fresh investment call. 

1) what is your time horizon
2) what are your return expectations 
3) what is your propensity to bear losses in the near term ?

items.caption_text:
Let’s understand what happened.

Oracle laid off 30,000 employees globally on Tuesday morning, roughly 18% of its global workforce, via a single email sent at 6am EST with no prior warning. 

System access was revoked almost immediately after. The cuts are expected to free up $8-10 billion in cash flow. 

Oracle’s stock has lost more than half its value since September 2025 and the company now carries over $124 billion in debt, up from $89 billion a year ago, with free cash flow running negative $10 billion last quarter.

What this means : 
Oracle posted a 95% jump in net income last quarter and still eliminated 18% of its workforce by email before most people finished their morning coffee. This is not a company in distress in the traditional sense. It’s a company that made an enormous debt-funded bet on AI infrastructure and is now converting its workforce into cash flow to service that debt.

Last month, I pulled in INR 11.6 Lakhs just by cashflowing my portfolio.

Think of your stocks like a piece of land. If the land just sits there, your rental income is zero. It’s growing in value, but it’s not putting cash in your pocket today. But what if you built a house on it? Now you’ve got rent coming in every single month. 🏠💰

That’s exactly how I treat my holdings in companies like Microsoft and Meta. 

This isn't about high-stakes gambling, it’s about using low-risk strategies that the wealthy have used for decades. 
It takes some time to learn, but once you treat your portfolio like a rental property, the game changes.

Watch the full strategy breakdown, link in bio

[AkshatShrivastava,  WisdomHatch,  investing,  cashflowing,  options]

If you are a long-term investor, buy stocks in risk-off phases.  

- Risk-On phase: high liquidity, low interest rates, full employment, government spending more, elections nearby, easy lending 

- Risk-off phases: low liquidity, moderate to high interest rates, jobs cuts, government cutting spending, banks being conservative 

We are in a risk-off phase of the market right now. This is when you get the best deals. 
However, the downside is: that if you buy today, your portfolio can continue to suffer until the time risk-off phase continues. 

If you are willing to go through the blood bath, you will make money. Else, you are better off investing in Metals/Real Estate.

​Ready to see which stock I’m betting on for 2026? 

Check the link in the bio!

#wisdomhatch #investing ​

Think tariffs only hurt the buyer? Think again. 🤔

A lot of people believe that an 18% tariff on Indian goods in the US will only be paid by American customers. But that's not the whole story. 📉
Think of it like GST. While it's technically paid by the customer, a sudden price hike can lead to lower demand, hitting producers and shopkeepers just as hard. 🛍️

In the same way, Indian producers will likely absorb some of the tariff costs to stay competitive, potentially eating into their profits. 📉
It's a complex economic issue with far-reaching consequences. 

What are your thoughts on this trade war? Let's discuss this in the comments! 👇

#Tariffs #TradeWar #wisdomhatch #IndiaUSRelations # GST

items.caption_text:
The clock hit 10.45PM, I had already beaten the crowd to reach Burj Khalifa.

I saw the crowd: looked like all the tourists from Goa had come to Dubai.

As we waited for the clock to strike 12. And the fireworks to start, I realised that each person standing here would waste 1 hour 15 minutes of their life just WAITING!

The wait seemed endless.

Is that how I want to waste the last few minutes of 2024?

NO.

So I cut my adventure short, came back and slept.

I was up at 6AM.

With an hour of workout I already felt energised.

High protein breakfast. Brisk walk. Meditation. All check.

By 9AM my day was already conquered.

What a great start to the New Year.

All achieved, why? because I chose purpose over party.

I am just kidding.

I got up at 9.30am. Kids are still asleep.

And, we had a great time last night.

My wife (@ayushichand ) and I also made sure that kids stayed up. And, watched the New Year show.

Taking life easy and creating memories is the biggest joy. And, it’s okay not to do anything productive the 1st day of the year:)

Out of 10 brands that approach Amrita Thakur, she turns down 6 or 7. Just like that.  Not because the money isn’t good, but because the ask is unrealistic. 

Three outfits. Sixty seconds. Price, fabric, sale date, brand story all in one reel. That’s not content. That’s a 2004 TVC in an Instagram format. 📱 

The best creators aren’t mannequins. They have a point of view, an aesthetic, and audience trust. ✨  The moment a 40-point brief overrides that, the content stops working. 

 Brands, take note: Less control. More trust. 🤝 🎙️ 

Featuring insights from Sakshi Dhingra, Arpan Biswas, CMO, Ajio, Vimarsh, Co-Founder & CEO, Underneath.in and Amrita Thakur, Style Curator & Fashion Designer.

items.caption_text:
A quick rapid fire, but it says everything.

From the obsession with followers to the overused “make the logo bigger,” from creators starting without a plan to brands chasing short-term wins—this is the reality of the creator economy today.

At the same time, there’s a shift worth noticing: consumers in Indian fashion are finally choosing self-expression, unapologetically.

But challenges remain—returns, noise, and a platform for everyone doesn’t always mean value for anyone.

Maybe the real fix is simple: build longer partnerships, back the right creators, and grow together.

Because sometimes, 60 seconds of honesty can reveal more than a 30-minute conversation. ⏱️✨

🎙️ Featuring insights from Sakshi Dhingra, Arpan Biswas,CMO, @AJIOLife, Vimarsh Razdan, CEO & Co-Founder, Underneat and Fashion Designer & Style Curator Amrita Thakur. 

#creatoreconomy #influencer #influencermarketing #Advertising #marketing mediabusiness businessofadvertising storyboard18 🚀

Getting back on the pole has been tough. I bruise more easily. My skin burns. Palms are super clammy. And I’ve been failing to do the moves I used to do easily. I’m also always on a timer with wanting to get back in time with Zay for dinner. Today there came a point when I said to myself I love pole and I can’t possibly be getting nothing. Maybe if I just slowed down and didn’t rush to the next move and just do this one which I’ve done so many times before - maybe just this for today will be good. 
And so, we did just that. Thank you @aarifapoleburnt for catching me always ❤️

You need to ask
Who is this for
Why should they care
What value does this create

Passion will get you started
Strategy will keep you relevant

The creators who win are not just passionate
They are intentional

Build with passion
But grow with strategy

items.caption_text:
In 2019, I was on a ventilator.

Thin kid. Junk food every day. Never thought twice about my health. Until my body completely shut down on me.
Woke up and the only thought was. Never again.

Started running. Started eating right. First real win: crossing the finish line of a 21km half marathon.
This weekend, my best friend and I ran Hyrox. Four hours of sleep, zero race-specific prep, and absolutely no regrets.
We crossed the finish line in 1 hour 40 minutes.

As a founder, the conversation is always about output, performance, execution.
Nobody talks about the machine behind all of it.

What helped us stay sharp through the race was keeping an eye on our vitals with Valour 1R- that made me realize the importance of tracking your progress and realizing your truest potential.

Super happy to be a part of the Valour athlete family!

#ValourAthlete

As a founder, I have realized this the hard way. You can get views, engagement, even sales, but if you cannot clearly track where it’s coming from, you are just guessing.

Influencer marketing is not broken, your measurement is. If you are not solving attribution, you are not scaling, you are just spending.

Instead of scrolling through endless chats, I now use @chatgptindia as my objective lens. It filters through the noise to find the patterns and gaps that the human eye misses when it’s too close to the work.

When you use AI to remove bias from your reviews, you stop managing through ego and start managing through data.

#chatgpt

items.caption_text:
Before you invest, do this first.

Most people lose money not because the idea was bad, but because they never understood the market.
Start with demand. Are people actively looking for this or are you forcing it? 

Study competitors. What are they doing right and where are they lacking? Talk to real users. Not your friends. Not your assumptions. Actual buyers. Check pricing. If people are not willing to pay, you don’t have a business. 
And most importantly, validate small before you scale big.

Market research is not a step. It is the difference between guessing and building something that actually works.

items.caption_text:
Success is rarely absolute.
It is always measured against a reference point.

If your reference point is comfort, small wins will feel like success.
If your reference point is survival, stability will feel like success.

But if your reference point is excellence, impact, and building something that outlives you… the bar automatically rises.

As founders, the biggest responsibility we have is not just building a business. It is choosing the reference point we measure our life against.

Set your bar where most people feel uncomfortable.
Set your bar where mediocrity cannot survive.
Set your bar where growth becomes the only option.

Because the higher your reference point, the higher your version of success becomes.

But if your reference point is excellence, impact, and building something that outlives you… the bar automatically rises.

As founders, the biggest responsibility we have is not just building a business. It is choosing the reference point we measure our life against.

Set your bar where most people feel uncomfortable.
Set your bar where mediocrity cannot survive.
Set your bar where growth becomes the only option.

Because the higher your reference point, the higher your version of success becomes.

items.caption_text:
When you become a manager, you stop practicing your skills and that’s a very big mistake.

Somewhere between meetings, deadlines, and managing people, you start believing your past expertise is enough. 

But the truth is, skills don’t stay where you left them. They either grow or they fade.
As a founder or leader, your role isn’t just to manage, it’s to stay sharp. Because the moment you disconnect from the craft, you lose your edge, your instincts, and eventually, your relevance.

The best leaders I’ve seen are still in the trenches. Still learning. Still practicing.

So no matter how senior you get, never stop doing the work.

Because leadership without skill is just noise

7 months ago, I randomly came across this tool while watching a Steven Bartlett podcast.

 He recommended it, I downloaded it the same day. Within a week I bought the paid subscription. No hesitation.

And honestly? It's one of the best decisions I've made for my productivity.

It's not a fancy AI tool. It doesn't do 100 things. It does one thing, voice to text. But it does it so insanely well that it has completely changed the way I work.

Every WhatsApp reply. Every email. Every Slack message. Every G-Chat. I just speak now. I don't type anymore.

It catches names correctly. It structures messages properly. It removes filler words. It just works across every single app on my phone and laptop.
And the time savings? I'm saving roughly 30 to 35 minutes every single day. That's 3+ hours a week. Just from not typing.

We never realize how much time we waste typing until something takes it away from you.

Apart from ChatGPT and Claude, if there's one tool worth paying for, this is it. Every working professional needs this in their life.

@wisprflow . Speak to your devices. Get everything done.
#ad

items.caption_text:
Obsession gets you started. Perspective takes you further.

I'm obsessive about what I build and I wear that proudly.

But here's what I've learned: my best ideas never came from a meeting room or a shoot. They came from a hotel lobby, a trip, a random Tuesday doing something completely unrelated.

The principle that defines everything I create? It came from the hospitality industry, simply because I chose to stay curious beyond my craft.
Obsession is the fuel. But a wider lens is the engine.
Because what gets you here won't always get you there.

Stay obsessed. But stay open

items.caption_text:
On Day 18 of our The Unlearn and Re-learn journey,
We come face to face with the fear that if we stop moving, we will be left behind.

That a pause means we are losing ground.
That stillness is something to be ashamed of.

So many of us learned to fear rest.
As if stopping erases progress.
As if slowing down means losing momentum.

But stillness is not a step backward.
It is a reset.
It is where the nervous system settles.

Where what you have lived through begins to make sense inside you.
Where your energy quietly rebuilds for what comes next.

Today, notice how life feels when you stop treating stillness as a delay and begin seeing it as a form of quiet preparation.

You are not falling behind when you pause.
You are allowing your life to catch up with your soul. 💛

items.caption_text:
On Day 17 of our The Unlearn and Re-learn journey,
Let us pause with the belief that we are behind simply because our life does not look like someone else’s by now.

→ We often turn timing into a race
→ We compare
→ We forget that every path has its own rhythm

But your pace is not random.
It is shaped by what you are learning and what you are healing.
 
Slowness is not failure. It is part of how real change settles in.

Today, notice what stirs inside you when your pace feels different.
→ The doubt
→ The pressure
→ Or the quiet relief of moving in your own time

You are not behind.
You are exactly where your life needs you to be.
And your journey is unfolding at the pace your becoming requires. 🩷

items.caption_text:
On Day 16 of our The Unlearn and Re-learn journey,

Take a moment to sense how your nervous system leans toward certainty before allowing itself to settle.
So often, we believe that calm will come once everything is predictable.

Once plans are clear.
Once nothing feels out of place.

As if safety only lives in a future where nothing can go wrong.
But safety is not created by controlling every outcome.
It begins in the small moments where you let your breath settle.

Where you remind your body that it is okay to not have all the answers.
Where you notice that even in uncertainty, you are still standing.

Today, gently notice what helps you feel even a little more relaxed when life feels out of control.
 
Not to fix the uncertainty. Only to remind your body that it can meet this moment, just as it is.

You do not need a perfect future to feel safe.
Sometimes, safety is simply trusting that you can take the next moment as it comes. 💛

items.caption_text:
On Day 15 of our The Unlearn and Re-learn journey, 
We touch something that quietly weighs on many hearts: the belief that one fall erases everything you have ever built. 

When life shakes you, there is a voice that whispers you are back to nothing, that your healing was a lie. 
But growth is not as fragile as your mind wants you to believe. It lives in your bones now. 

It is woven into the way you pause before reacting, the way you catch yourself mid-spiral, the way you even notice you are struggling. 

That awareness did not exist before. 
That is proof that your work is still alive. 

Setbacks do not delete your journey. 
They simply test whether you will abandon yourself when the road gets rough. 

Today, listen closely to the voice that speaks when you are down. 
Is it the voice of someone who loves you, or someone waiting for you to fail? 

The words you choose in your lowest moments become the foundation you rebuild from. 
And often, the version of you that rises after a fall carries a wisdom the untested version never could. 💛

items.caption_text:
On Day 14 of our The Unlearn and Re-learn journey,
I invite you to notice how often we hide what we feel in the name of keeping love peaceful and undisturbed.

So many of us learned that showing discomfort means creating a problem.

That speaking our truth might threaten the connection.
That silence is safer than honesty.

But love does not grow through hiding.
It also grows through truth.

Through allowing emotions to be seen, even when they feel heavy.
Through trusting that honesty does not break the bond, but deepens it.

Today, you may quietly notice what happens inside you when your feelings want to be expressed, but you choose to stay silent.

And you may begin to sense how being real, little by little, brings more calm than fear ever could.
Love does not ask you to disappear. It asks you to arrive as you are. 💛

items.caption_text:
On Day 13 of our The Unlearn and Re-learn journey,

I invite you to notice the belief that holding on tighter is the only way to prove you care, and that letting go must mean you are uncaring.

Many of us learned to stay attached even when the weight becomes heavy.

To hold longer than our heart feels able to.
To confuse endurance with love.
But letting go with a full heart is not rejection.

→ It is a quiet form of respect.
→ It is release without bitterness.
→ It is love that knows when to soften its grip.

Today, notice how your body feels when something you are holding has begun to feel too heavy.

There is a strength that does not come from holding tighter.
 
It comes from releasing with love and trusting yourself to keep walking forward. 💛

items.caption_text:
On Day 12 of our The Unlearn and Re-learn journey,
We sit with a belief many of us carry quietly: that our growth only matters when someone else sees it.

That our healing must be visible.
That our progress must be witnessed.
That we are becoming only when we are acknowledged.

But so much of real change happens where no one is looking.

→ In the way you speak to yourself inside.
→ In the pause, you take instead of reacting.
→ In the strength it takes to choose differently, even in silence.

Today, you may gently notice how you recognize your own quiet progress.
Not for the world.
Not for approval.
Simply for yourself.

Some of the most important parts of your becoming will never be announced.
They will simply live inside you, and that is more than enough. 💛

items.caption_text:
On Day 11 of our The Unlearn and Re-learn journey, 

We rest our attention on how we move through change.

So often, there is an unspoken rush to “be okay already,” to adjust quickly, to prove that change is not affecting us too deeply. 

But the body does not work on deadlines. It listens for safety first. It waits for steadiness before it opens.
When you move slowly, you are not falling behind.

You are letting your nervous system understand what is happening.
You are giving yourself a chance to feel supported from the inside.

Today, notice the pace your body naturally chooses when something is shifting.
Not the pace you think you should have.
Just the one that feels calm enough to breathe in.

Let change meet you where you are, not where you think you should already be. 💛

items.caption_text:
On Day 10 of our The Unlearn and Re-learn journey,

We look at a belief many of us learned quietly: that we should keep going no matter how tired, tight, or uncomfortable we feel.

We learn to ignore our body.
We learn to push past tiredness.
We learn to keep moving even when something inside asks us to stop.

But your body is not trying to stop you.
It is trying to care for you.
It speaks through small signs.

→ Through aches
→ Through tightness
→ Through a heaviness that only wants rest and kindness

Today, there is nothing to fix.
Nothing to prove.

Only a gentle moment to listen.
What has your body been trying to tell you?

You do not have to force yourself to deserve rest.
Your body is not weak for speaking.
It has been trying to protect you all along. 💛

items.caption_text:
On Day 9 of our The Unlearn and Re-learn journey,

Today, we hold space for the part of us that over-explains our boundaries in the search for acceptance.

For a long time, many of us learned to open every door inside us just so we would not feel misunderstood.

We shared more than we were ready to share.
We crossed our own limits just to keep the peace.

But your inner world is not meant to be open to everyone.
Some parts of you are sacred.
Only you get to decide what feels safe to share and what needs your protection.

Today, gently notice what moves inside you when you think about setting a boundary.
Do your thoughts rush to explain?
Do you feel fear of being misunderstood?
Or does a quiet strength begin to form?

There is no right response here.
Only an invitation to honor what you feel and stand with yourself, softly and firmly. 💛

items.caption_text:
On Day 8 of our The Unlearn and Re-learn journey, 

Let us pause and notice how often we wait to feel completely ready before we allow ourselves to move forward.

That pause where you wonder if you are ready yet.

If you are steady enough.
If you are sure enough.

So many of us learned to wait for confidence to arrive first.
To wait for certainty to settle everything.
To wait until the fear disappears.

But often, confidence is not something you find before you move.
It is something that slowly forms because you moved.

Today, notice what shifts inside you when you take even the smallest step without knowing exactly how it will unfold.
Notice what softens.
Notice what steadies.

Sometimes the path becomes visible only after your foot touches the ground. 💛

items.caption_text:
On Day 7 of our The Unlearn and Re-learn journey,
 
We return to a belief many of us still hold without realizing it: that an emotion is valid only if we can explain it.
For so long, we have been taught to question what we feel, to look for a reason before letting the feeling exist.

But some emotions rise without a story.
They surface simply because something inside you wants to be acknowledged.

Instead of trying to justify them, imagine offering these feelings a quiet place to rest.
Notice how your body shifts when an emotion appears with no clear cause.
Can you stay with it gently, without asking it to make sense first?

A feeling does not need proof to matter.
It is enough because it belongs to you. 💛

items.caption_text:
On Day 6 of our The Unlearn and Re-learn journey,

I invite you to notice how deeply you have been taught that productivity means doing more, faster, and without pause.

There is a quiet exhaustion that comes from always trying to stay ahead of life.
From always bracing for what is next.

Over time, the body forgets what it feels like to move without tension.
Today is not about changing your habits.
 
It is about noticing how you move through the moment you are already in.

→ With tightness
→ With ease
→ With holding
→ With release

Let this be a small return to yourself.

To how your breath naturally wants to flow.
To how your body asks to be met.
You do not need to push this moment away for the next one to arrive. 💛

items.caption_text:
On Day 5 of our The Unlearn and Re-learn journey,

It brings us into the calm part of life we often avoid the space where we do not yet know what comes next.

Not knowing can make the body tighten and the mind prepare for the worst.

It can feel like the ground beneath you is shifting without warning, as if something difficult is already on its way.

But the unknown is not always a sign of danger.

It can also be the first hint that life is changing in ways you have not yet discovered or imagined, a space holding growth and new possibilities.

Today, simply notice what moves through you when the future feels blurry.

Do your thoughts reach for control, or do they pause long enough to sense what might be forming?

There is no right reaction here.
Only an invitation to meet your inner world with a little more softness as it learns to breathe in uncertainty. 💛

items.caption_text:
On Day 4 of our The Unlearn and Re-learn journey

I invite you to slow your pace and make room for what feels uncomfortable.

When difficult emotions rise, the nervous system often tightens.
It looks for control, for distraction, for a way out.

But emotions do not soften through fixing.
They soften when they feel safe enough to stay.
They soften in the presence.

Today, let us pause for a moment and gently notice what is moving within us.
Not with urgency.
Not with judgment.

Just with steady awareness.

items.caption_text:
On Day 3 of our The Unlearn and Re-learn journey,

We pause to understand something tender and very human: the need for space.

When someone asks for space, it is natural to feel unsure or to wonder if you did something wrong.
Your mind may try to fill the silence or hold on tightly.

But space is not the absence of care.
It is a quiet moment that helps people breathe, settle their feelings, and return with a clearer heart.

Today, gently notice what happens inside you when distance appears.

Does your body tense up?
Does your heart search for answers?

Whatever you find, meet it with softness.

These feelings come from your story, and they deserve patience and kindness.

And often, the connections that matter grow stronger when you honour the space that helps them breathe. 💛

items.caption_text:
The Unlearn & Re-learn journey begins today, a space to slow down and reconnect with the parts of you that got buried under pace, pressure, and expectation.

Most people imagine change arriving in big, dramatic moments.
But it usually begins quietly, in the moment you realise a belief you’ve carried no longer fits the person you’re becoming.

Beliefs like:

“I should always be okay.”
“Rest makes me fall behind.”
“My feelings are too much.”

Each day, we’ll take one of these old beliefs, gently loosen the conditioning around it, and re-learn a version that feels steadier, kinder, and more aligned with who you are now.

This isn’t about fixing.
It’s about returning to clarity, softness, and the inner voice that has always been there.

✨ Day 1 begins with silence, not emptiness, but the place where your nervous system finally exhales.
And in that small pause, your inner world finally finds room to breathe again.

#unlearnrelearn #innerwork #mindfulnessjourney #healingenergy #selfhealers #slowdownmovement #somatichealing

items.caption_text:
#5 is the hardest for me right now 😭 This is the decade where small choices become your whole lifestyle. ✨

1. They manage access, not time.
Their calendar is protected because their attention is valuable. Not everyone gets a front-row seat to their mind.
2. They’re boring in the best way.
The glow-up isn’t a mood, it’s repetition. Same sleep window. Same workouts. Same money habits. Same work blocks.
3. They don’t argue with reality, they negotiate with it.
Not “this shouldn’t be happening” — but “what is this teaching me, and what’s my next move?”
4. They’re not always confident. They’re committed.
They’ve mastered showing up even when they feel messy, uncertain, or unprepared.
5. They treat energy like a resource, not a vibe.
Fewer late nights. Less chaos. Less people-pleasing. A calm nervous system is a power move.
6. They ask better questions.
Not “will this work?” but “what would make this work?” Not “is this risky?” but “what’s the cheapest way to test this?”
7. They’re obsessive about what they consume.
Not just food — content, conversations, music, news, even jokes. Mental junk quietly becomes your personality.
8. They don’t chase opportunities. They become referable.
Deliver on time. Follow through. Be easy to work with. Trust travels faster than talent.
9. They don’t confuse busy with valuable.
Power is choosing the one thing that moves everything, then doing it daily. Busy is often just avoidance wearing makeup.
10. They’re not afraid to outgrow people.
No drama. But it’s final. They choose environments that match their future, not their past.

items.caption_text:
Comment AI and I’ll send you the link.

Most organizations are stuck in the early stages of AI adoption. 

Having implemented AI solutions for over 50 enterprises globally, I have identified five distinct levels of AI maturity that define how a business scales its productivity and automation.

Level 1: Basic Adoption
Teams use ChatGPT or Claude for isolated tasks like writing emails. This is where most businesses currently reside.

Level 2: The Bot Stage
Departments build custom GPTs or Claude Projects for specific, repetitive tasks.

Level 3: Vibe Coding and Generative Design
Marketing teams generate AI creatives while other departments use vibe coding to build functional dashboards and interfaces.

Level 4: Connected AI Workflows
Organizations move beyond silos. They begin connecting different applications to build cohesive AI workflows that streamline data across the company.

Level 5: The Agent Stage
The peak of AI integration. Businesses build autonomous systems and AI agents that run entire processes with minimal intervention.

If you are unsure where your organization stands in this evolution, we can help you evaluate your current AI strategy and technical stack.

items.caption_text:
Most large enterprises face the same bottleneck: they are sitting on massive amounts of data but lack the workflows to utilize it effectively. 

We see this gap constantly.

We recently solved this for a major conglomerate in Sri Lanka. 

Their procurement team was receiving multiple agency proposals every week, and each time, they started from scratch. 

They were manually researching vendors and trying to recall past collaborations from memory. It was a highly inefficient manual process.

We transformed this using AI automation. Now, the moment a proposal arrives in an inbox, the AI workflow takes over. 

The reality is that a human cannot process, synthesize, and analyze that volume of data manually. 

AI excels at this exact task. To implement AI workflows in your own business, you do not need to start with something complex.

items.caption_text:
Buying vs Renting 🏡💸 — which one is really better?

Buying a home means building equity, enjoying stability, and potentially earning from property appreciation. But it also comes with mortgages, taxes, and maintenance costs.

Renting gives you flexibility, lower upfront costs, and less responsibility for repairs. But the money you pay each month doesn’t turn into an asset, and long-term, you miss out on investment growth.

So, should you buy or rent? It depends on your financial goals, lifestyle, and long-term plans. For some, owning is freedom. For others, renting is peace of mind.

What’s your pick? 🤔

#BuyVsRent #HomeDecisions #FinancialFreedom #InvestSmart #RealEstateTips MoneyMatters”

In 2026 don’t lose out on personal finance. Start your personal finance journey with this podcast! 🤑💸

In this episode, finance experts Gajendra Kothari (@iamoneidiot), Sanjay Kathuria (@financebysanjay), and Kirttan Shah (@kirtanshahcfp) talk about what real wealth actually means and how it is built over time. They break down how much money is truly “enough,” the role of mutual funds, equities, ETFs, SIP versus lump-sum investing, and why asset allocation matters most during bear markets. The discussion goes beyond numbers into investor psychology, fear cycles, EMI traps, and the importance of having the right financial guidance. They also examine how geopolitics, US tariffs, and RBI’s gold strategy influence markets, explore the future of money through gold, silver, and digital assets, and close with practical advice, book recommendations, and a grounded outlook on the markets ahead making this episode a concise blueprint for anyone serious about long-term wealth creation.

items.caption_text:
Everyone wants high returns with low risk—but that combination doesn’t exist.

The truth?
Investing becomes dangerous only when you don’t know your risk profile.
Stop copying your neighbour’s returns or some expert’s volatility tolerance.
This journey is a single-player game, and your only benchmark is you.

Find your place on the risk scale.
Be honest about your expectations.
And build wealth at your pace—safely and sustainably. 🚀📈

We’re never gonna survive 💅 
@natasha.poonawalla for @kingstrust in New York! 
With @rheakapoor @iambidipto_ @sanyakapoor 

Repost/On this Spring evening in New York, @natasha.poonawalla wears a custom warm ivory muga silk sari ensemble by @arpita__mehta , hand-embroidered with Kantha.

Kantha, an ancient, eco-conscious embroidery tradition from Bengal, where old saris and dhotis were repurposed into layered textiles. In this ensemble, that philosophy is preserved, bringing together fragments of lived histories with a fresh, contemporary perspective.

The look is completed with custom earrings by @shriparamanijewels in collaboration with Arpita Mehta, paired with traditional Indian jadau jewellery.

Navratan bajubandh (right) - @themedjewellerybypoojanigam
Hair pin - @jagdishjewellerschandigarh
Emerald choker, emerald earrings, navratan bajubandh and peacock ring - @archanaaggarwalofficial 
Croc kada - @rejagems 
Tiger kada and peacock kada - @thehouseoframbhajos 
Polki and ruby ring - @raniwala1881
custom clutch @meera_mahadevia
Draped by @a__patel

items.caption_text:
For my beautiful Radhika Ambani, custom Chanel Haute couture referencing their iconic Paris-Bombay Pre Fall 2011 one of my favourite collections of all time.

This love affair between paris and bombay, Chanel and India has always lived through craft, through the hands of artisans, and through a shared respect for heritage and detail. And you can still feel it now.

And that’s how this story continues. Through craft, through appreciation, and through a new generation, who carries it forward with curiosity and respect.

South sea pearl accents on the tweed and navratna buttons by genius @tarang_arora @amrapalijewels 
Custom couture shoes by Chanel and hand embroidered silk bag by @anamikakhanna.in 

captured by @farhanhussain

items.caption_text:
For @bvlgari Parfum, @kareenakapoorkhan wears custom @hannahkhiangteofficial and @christianlouboutin with Bvlgari Jewels. 

Hannah Khiangte is a designer from Aizawl, Mizoram, who launched her label in 2013 to spotlight the intricate puan weaves of her heritage. Her brand works closely with Mizo women artisans, blending tradition with contemporary design. Together, they preserve and modernize Mizo textile culture for a global audience.

This look draws inspiration from the traditional Mizo puan, a treasured textile that symbolizes identity, heritage, and celebration. Handwoven by women artisans, the garment blends classic motifs with androgynous tailoring and punk-era influences. Designed with Kareena in mind, it reflects her bold, evolving style while spotlighting the rich cultural legacy of Mizoram. Showcasing the puan on Kareena we bring this heirloom to a global stage with pride and purpose.

styled with @shereenlovebug 
beauty @savleenmanchanda and @miteshrajani 
captured @anubhav_sood 
my team @stylebyvanshika @ashitabhamri
